description: Add E2E test for the npm auto-discovery pipeline — the primary mechanism by which production users discover plugins. Currently entirely skipped in E2E tests via OPENTABS_SKIP_NPM_DISCOVERY=1.

userStories.title: Add E2E test for npm plugin auto-discovery
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want an E2E test that exercises the npm auto-discovery pipeline so that regressions in the primary plugin discovery mechanism are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Test verifies that npm-installed plugins are discovered when OPENTABS_SKIP_NPM_DISCOVERY is not set
Test verifies discovered npm plugins appear in /health endpoint with source='npm'
Test verifies npm plugin tools are callable via MCP tool dispatch
Test verifies local plugins override npm plugins of the same name
Test handles the case where no npm plugins are installed (empty discovery)
userStories.notes: This is the highest-impact missing E2E test. All existing E2E tests set OPENTABS_SKIP_NPM_DISCOVERY=1 which completely bypasses npm resolution. The npm discovery pipeline is in platform/mcp-server/src/resolver.ts and platform/mcp-server/src/discovery.ts. The test needs to: (1) install a test plugin globally or into a controlled node_modules, (2) start the MCP server without OPENTABS_SKIP_NPM_DISCOVERY, (3) verify the plugin appears in /health. Look at e2e/helpers/mcp-server.ts for the startMcpServer helper — it currently sets OPENTABS_SKIP_NPM_DISCOVERY='1'. You may need to add a variant or option that skips this env var. The challenge is making npm discovery work in CI without polluting global node_modules — consider using NODE_PATH or npm link to a temporary directory. Use opus because this requires understanding the discovery pipeline and creating a reliable test fixture. The test file should be e2e/npm-discovery.e2e.ts. Check e2e/discovery-edge-cases.e2e.ts for patterns around plugin discovery testing.
